title: muehlenbeckia gracillima
intro: Muehlenbeckia gracillima, also known as the 'Ngaio' or 'Five-Leaf Muehlenbeckia', is a small, woody climber native to New Zealand and Australia, belonging to the family Polygonaceae. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Muehlenbeckia gracillima have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Muehlenbeckia gracillima include its potential to help reduce fever, alleviate colds and flu, and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Muehlenbeckia gracillima is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be dried and used in smoking blends for its perceived respiratory benefits.
